name: Bernedo and Puerto de la Aldea Loop
name_original: Mirafuentes - Bernedo por el puerto de La Aldea
description: This difficult 34.3-mile road cycling route in Sierra de Codés offers varied landscapes and low traffic, climbing to Puerto de La Aldea.
region_intro: Road cycling around Mirafuentes, situated in Navarre, Spain, provides access to diverse terrain at the foot of the Sierra de Codés mountain range. The region features mountainous landscapes, rolling hills, and oak groves, offering a mix of demanding ascents and quiet roads. Cyclists can experience varied topography, including views of the Ebro Valley, ensuring a range of experiences. Best road cycling routes around Mirafuentes The most popular road cycling route is Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo, a 70.1 miles (112.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bernedo and Puerto de la Aldea Loop, a difficult 34.3 miles (55.2 km) path. This route includes the climb towards Puerto de La Aldea, known for its scenic views and light traffic. Local road cyclists also love the Irache Fountain – Plaza de San Martín loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu, a 39.7 miles (63.8 km) trail leading through rolling hills and agricultural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Mirafuentes is defined by mountainous terrain, rolling hills, and quiet roads, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Mirafuentes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 3 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mirafuentes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Mirafuentes?
A: There are nearly 30 road cycling routes around Mirafuentes, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes of varying difficulty, from easy to difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mirafuentes?
A: The region around Mirafuentes, particularly the Sierra de Codés, experiences snow on its summits in winter. Therefore, spring, summer, and autumn generally offer the most favorable conditions for road cycling, with pleasant temperatures and clear roads for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Mirafuentes?
A: Yes, while many routes feature challenging climbs, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. The area also offers paths through rolling hills and agricultural areas that can be more approachable for those new to road cycling.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Mirafuentes?
A: Road cycling around Mirafuentes offers a mix of terrain. You'll find mountainous landscapes, rolling hills, and oak groves, particularly at the foot of the Sierra de Codés. Routes can include demanding ascents, such as the climb towards Puerto de La Aldea, and more gentle paths through agricultural areas, with views extending to the Ebro Valley.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes in the Mirafuentes area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986763909/bernedo-and-puerto-de-la-aldea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bernedo and Puerto de la Aldea Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 55.2 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20164998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irache Fountain – Plaza de San Martín loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 63.8 km ride through rolling hills.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers stunning natural beauty. You can find routes with views of the Ebro Valley and the Sierra de Codés. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1255178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7580262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Plana Summit (1,338 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5320730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Dormida Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Mirafuentes is known for its demanding ascents. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984876927/ibernalo-iturrieta-hermitage-of-santa-teodesia-estella-monastery-of-irache-return-from-acedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo&lt;/a&gt;, a 112.8 km trail with significant elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986763909/bernedo-and-puerto-de-la-aldea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bernedo and Puerto de la Aldea Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the climb to Puerto de La Aldea, are considered difficult and ideal for advanced cyclists.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Mirafuentes?
A: The road cycling routes around Mirafuentes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from mountainous climbs to rolling hills, and the tranquility of the quiet roads, which are less trafficked.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through historical or cultural landmarks?
A: Yes, the broader Navarre region, accessible from Mirafuentes, is rich in history. Routes can lead through medieval towns and agricultural areas, adding a cultural dimension to your ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984876927/ibernalo-iturrieta-hermitage-of-santa-teodesia-estella-monastery-of-irache-return-from-acedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, passes by the Monastery of Irache.

Q: Are there any specific climbs or passes that are popular among road cyclists?
A: The climb towards Puerto de La Aldea is a notable feature, known for its scenic views and light traffic, and is included in routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986763909/bernedo-and-puerto-de-la-aldea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bernedo and Puerto de la Aldea Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The ascent to the Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Codés, though short, is also a demanding climb with sections reaching an 11% incline.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take to complete in Mirafuentes?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984876927/ibernalo-iturrieta-hermitage-of-santa-teodesia-estella-monastery-of-irache-return-from-acedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo&lt;/a&gt; route, at 112.8 km, typically takes around 5 hours 30 minutes, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20164998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irache Fountain – Plaza de San Martín loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu&lt;/a&gt; (63.8 km) can be completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes.

Q: Is there information available about public transport access to road cycling routes in Mirafuentes?
A: While the region has a strong local cycling heritage and well-maintained roads, specific information about public transport access directly to trailheads is not detailed. Cyclists typically start their rides from Mirafuentes or nearby towns, which may have local bus services. For detailed public transport options, it's advisable to consult local transport authorities in Navarre.
tips: The 16th century sanctuary and guesthouse complex are located on top of a hillside facing the Ebro valley in the border area between Navarra (to which it belongs), Álava and La Rioja. From the outside, the view of the ridges towards the Ebro valley recalls the privileged sites for spiritual life where sanctuaries and monasteries were built.

Lapobación is a medieval settlement that probably arose from its location in the natural passage between the rocky ridges of limestone between the north and the south. It is part of a municipality made up of Lapoblación and Meano. From the road, the gray limestone ridges that hang over the core of old stone houses can be seen.
It acts as a "portillo" between valleys. Too bad you have to cross it on the same road.
Small town at the foot of the northern slope of the port of La Aldea,.... a classic in the area

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Mirafuentes - Bernedo cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the villages of Mirafuentes or Bernedo, which serve as the start and end points of this route. As it's a point-to-point route, consider logistics for returning to your starting point or arranging a pick-up.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Mirafuentes - Bernedo route?
faqs.answer: The pass to Puerto de La Aldea remains open year-round. However, short closures can occur in winter due to heavy snowfall. For the most pleasant experience, spring and autumn offer ideal temperatures and conditions for road cycling in this region.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route in Sierra de Codés?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Mirafuentes - Bernedo route or to access the Sierra de Codés area along this public road.

faqs.question: Is the Mirafuentes - Bernedo cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions, this is a road cycling route, which is generally not ideal for dogs unless they are accustomed to running alongside a bike for long distances or can comfortably ride in a specialized trailer. Always prioritize your pet's safety and comfort on such routes.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Mirafuentes - Bernedo cycling route?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as difficult. It covers 55.2 km with 1216 meters of elevation gain. The ascent to Puerto de La Aldea is described as a "very steady climb," allowing for a comfortable rhythm, but it still requires good fitness for the duration and elevation.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain and picturesque scenery. You'll pass through varied landscapes, including the medieval Lapoblación Village, and ascend to Puerto de La Aldea. The southern side of La Aldea is particularly scenic, dominated by the vineyards of the Rioja-Alavesa region, while the Cantabrian Mountains provide a dramatic backdrop to the north. The road surface is reported to be in excellent condition throughout.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints are along the Mirafuentes - Bernedo route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the 16th-century Sanctuary of Our Lady of Codés with views of the Ebro valley, the charming Lapoblación Village, and the impressive vistas from Puerto de La Aldea. The route also passes through Bernedo Village.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the [CIMA AL06] Bernedo (La Aldea), Sendero Histórico: Navarra, and the Ignatian Way. You may also encounter parts of the Bernedo-Angostina-Quintana-Urturi and Ibilbide historikoa: Araba trails.

faqs.question: Is there much traffic on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: A significant advantage of this trail for road cyclists is the very low traffic volume. This contributes to a more peaceful and enjoyable ride, making it a popular choice among local cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through villages like Lapoblación and Bernedo, specific cafes or refreshment stops are not detailed. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the climb to Puerto de La Aldea, and plan stops in the villages.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a bike trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, pack essentials like a repair kit, spare tube, pump, sufficient water, and snacks. Dress in appropriate cycling attire, including layers for changing weather, and always wear a helmet. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Codés
